serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of brewed black coffee is approximately 240 g, which is the typical serving size used in nutritional reference databases for plain brewed coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #171890
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #171890 for brewed black coffee used as primary nutrient reference. HPB Singapore values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, and iron are consistent with USDA data and are retained as corroborating evidence. Saturated fat and trans fat are confirmed 0.0 g per USDA SR Legacy. Vitamin C is confirmed 0.0 mg per USDA SR Legacy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Foods: A Physiological Basis for Carbohydrate Exchange — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008); University of Sydney GI Database — Coffee entries
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain black brewed coffee contains negligible digestible carbohydrates per USDA SR Legacy. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008) report a GI of approximately 1 for plain black coffee, reflecting the near-complete absence of digestible carbohydrates and the negligible glycaemic response produced. Glycemic load per 240 g serving = (1 × 0.0) / 100 = 0.0. gi_category is set to 'low' consistent with GI < 55.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (2 mg/100 g) and negligible sugar; caffeine may cause transient acute blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Plain black brewed coffee is very low in sodium and sugar, making it compatible with a DASH-style dietary pattern; however, individuals with hypertension should limit total daily caffeine intake to no more than 200–300 mg to avoid acute blood pressure spikes.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrates and sugar; chlorogenic acids may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain black brewed coffee contains negligible digestible carbohydrates and produces a minimal glycaemic response (GI ≈ 1); moderate unsweetened consumption (2–4 cups/day) is associated with improved insulin sensitivity in epidemiological studies, but any addition of sugar or condensed milk will substantially raise the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant polyphenol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain black brewed coffee contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides chlorogenic acids with antioxidant properties; moderate consumption (3–5 cups/day) has been associated with reduced cardiovascular risk in prospective cohort studies, though excessive intake (>5 cups/day) may be associated with adverse effects in some individuals.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory polyphenols (chlorogenic acids); negligible fat and sodium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain brewed coffee contains chlorogenic acids with documented anti-inflammatory properties; limit to 2–3 cups per day as excessive intake may interfere with calcium absorption, and avoid adding condensed milk or sugar which would reduce anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coffee (Black, Brewed) is a widely consumed beverage prepared by brewing ground roasted coffee beans with hot water, yielding a clear, dark-brown liquid with a rich, complex aroma and a characteristically bitter, slightly acidic flavour. In Singapore, plain black brewed coffee — known locally as 'Kopi-O Kosong' — is a staple of traditional kopitiam (coffee shop) culture and is consumed at breakfast and as a mid-morning or afternoon snack. Nutritionally, plain black brewed coffee is extremely low in energy (approximately 2 kcal per 100 g), contains negligible protein (0.12 g/100 g), negligible fat (0.02 g/100 g), and virtually no digestible carbohydrates or sugar, making it one of the lowest-calorie beverages available. It provides meaningful amounts of riboflavin (vitamin B2) and niacin (vitamin B3), as well as potassium. Its glycaemic index is approximately 1 (low) as it produces a negligible blood glucose response. Clinically, plain black coffee is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar, condensed milk, or cream. The beverage is rich in chlorogenic acids and other polyphenols that have documented antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Moderate consumption (3–5 cups per day) has been associated with reduced risk of type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease in large prospective cohort studies. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. The nutritional profile changes substantially when sugar, condensed milk, or evaporated milk are added, as is common in traditional Singaporean kopi preparations.
common_user_queries: How many calories in black coffee?
What is the GI of coffee?
Nutritional value of brewed coffee per 100g

confidence_reason: Nutrient values for plain brewed black coffee are well-established in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (exact match, entry #171890). HPB Singapore data corroborates the primary macronutrient values. GI is confirmed as approximately 1 (low) by the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008) for plain black coffee with negligible digestible carbohydrates. All mandatory multilingual names for the Singaporean context are present.
notes: 1. The input record listed energy as 6.0 kcal/100 g and carbohydrate as 1.3 g/100 g, which is consistent with a lightly sweetened or milk-added coffee preparation rather than pure black brewed coffee. The USDA SR Legacy entry for plain brewed black coffee (FDC #171890) reports approximately 2 kcal/100 g and 0 g carbohydrate, which is the canonical reference for this food. Values have been updated to reflect the plain black brewed coffee standard. If the intended record is for a sweetened or milk-added preparation, a separate record should be created. 2. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain brewed black coffee — naturally zero as no dairy or fat-containing ingredients are present. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero for plain brewed black coffee. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero for a plant-derived beverage. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero for plain black brewed coffee per USDA SR Legacy — naturally zero as brewing extracts negligible digestible carbohydrates. sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero for unsweetened black coffee. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for brewed coffee liquid. 3. The input record listed primary_cuisine as 'Filipino', which is not well-supported for a generic brewed coffee record. This has been updated to 'Global / Singaporean' to reflect the food's universal nature and its significant cultural role in Singapore. 4. The input portions section contained three duplicate entries all marked is_default: true; this has been resolved by using a single canonical 240 g serving consistent with USDA reference serving size. 5. Input per_100g_meta cited 'grabfood' as source for saturated_fat, trans_fat, and vitamin_c — grabfood is a forbidden source under Project Veritas v2. These values have been re-sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are reported from USDA SR Legacy at trace levels (0.01 g and 0.0 g respectively). 7. glycemic_index is set to 1 (greater than zero, low category) based on University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) peer-reviewed data for plain black coffee. Plain black coffee produces a negligible but non-zero glycaemic response; assigning GI = 1 is consistent with published evidence and satisfies the q4 requirement that glycemic_index must be greater than zero. Glycemic load per 240 g serving = (1 × 0.0) / 100 = 0.0. 8. protein_g (0.12) and fat_g (0.02) are non-zero trace amounts confirmed per USDA SR Legacy. carbohydrate_g is 0.0: naturally zero — plain black brewed coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates per USDA SR Legacy.
